column3: A curated physical artefact that contains or carries the sampled matter (S13). Serves as the object-management layer: it holds identifiers, condition records, custody, storage, and images. P46 is used as a pragmatic contains/carries proxy for collection management purposes.

The removed matter treated as an amount of matter (CRMsci S13) whose identity is grounded in material continuity and representativity. Serves as the scientific layer: it is the subject of measurement, observation, modification, and splitting events.

This model defines a Sample using a dual-layer structure: E22 (Physical Sample) is the curated physical artefact carrying persistent identifiers, custody, condition records, and images; S13 (Material Sample) is the removed matter that is the subject of scientific events. Both are created through a Sample Taking event. The E22 carries/contains the S13 via P46. Connections to other models (Heritage Object, Sample Site, analytical events) are distributed across both entities as semantically appropriate.
The event during which the sample was physically removed from its source object.
The event during which a new child sample was physically separated from the parent sample.
The legal owner of the sample.
The current location of the sample if different to its permanent location.
The organisation responsible for the care, storage, or management of the physical sample.
Primarily related to authored notes, reports and publications, however there is a whole range of relevant documents that can reference or be linked to this sample.

An observation or examination event related to the sample, ranging from simple visual inspection or interpretation to quantitative measurement or analytical investigation. These events may or may not result in recorded images, data, or other outputs. (S27 is a parent of the standard Core CRM Measurement)
An event that altered the sample after its initial removal, such as preparation, embedding, or coating.
The primary representative image of the curated physical sample.
Additional documentary images of the curated physical sample.

A named place where a sample is permanently stored, such as an institution, department, building, or room. Use this option when no physical storage unit record exists for the sample. If a storage unit is known, link to that instead and allow the location to be inherited through the unit's own records.

A discrete physical container in which a sample is stored, such as a cabinet, drawer, box, or tray. Storage units should themselves carry a permanent location (E53) or link to a larger containing unit, which in turn resolves to a named location. Units may be nested to any depth before resolving to a place. Use this option in preference to a direct location link when the specific unit is known.

column1: // --- Identifiers and labels (E22: Physical Sample carries persistent identity) ---

column3: For samples not defined with a fully resolvable PID like an IGSN the preferred ID is likely to be the same as or based on the E41: Sample Label/No/ID.
E41: Unique System Label or ID

column1: E41: Unique System Label or ID
column3: Required within some documentation or database systems, such as the ResearchSpace required label.

A human-readable name or number used to identify the sample in documentation or images, typically visible on labels or microscope slides. This field will generally need to follow an agreed local naming protocol.

column1: E41: Unique System Label or ID
column3: Automatic: Preferred Heritage Object ID - Sample Label
Any other identifier or code by which the sample may be known in external systems, publications, or legacy records. This also includes identifiers assigned by other systems and organisations.

column1: // --- Description (E22: Physical Sample as subject of documentation) ---

column3: This could include a whole range of descriptions relating to the sample.

column1: // --- Classification (E22: Physical Sample -- curation and format properties) ---

column3: The physical form of the sample as a material object. The format of the sample may change over time.
Keywords or classification terms used for discovery, retrieval, and contextualisation of the sample record (e.g., class, technique, composition).
The material or substance type of the removed matter (e.g. paint, ground, varnish, adhesive, metal, stone).

column1: // --- Dimensions and condition (E22: Physical Sample -- object-management layer) ---

column3:
Dimensions or size class of the sample, controlled list:
micro-sample (< 0.01 m),
small-sample (0.01 – 0.1 m),
medium-sample (0.1 – 0.3 m),
large-sample (0.3 – 1.0 m),
very-large-fragment (> 1.0 m)
The current condition, treatment state, or lifecycle position of a heritage sample, reflecting analytical preparation, handling, availability, or degree of alteration over time.

column1: // --- Scientific events (S13: Material Sample -- scientific layer) ---
// --- Sample Splitting (S13 is the source; both a new E22 and S13 are produced) ---

column3: A new curated Physical Sample identity created as the result of a Sample Splitting event. It represents a distinct registered sample entity derived from the source sample's material (S13), with its own persistent identity, storage, status, and lifecycle history.

The new material sample created through a Sample Splitting event. Although shown here only in relation to the splitting activity, this sub-sample is a full S13 Sample and may independently be observed, measured, modified, represented by images, and further split.

column1: // --- Heritage Object connection (via S2, not direct -- see workflow) ---

column3: The artwork or object from which the sample originated.
